attractions.name: African American Museum of Iowa
attractions.description: The African American Museum of Iowa is the only statewide museum dedicated exclusively to preserving, exhibiting, and teaching Iowa's African American history. Founded in 1993 and recently renovated, it serves as a leading educational resource that engages visitors from all backgrounds in conversations about community, race, and social justice.

highlights: Endless Possibilities: A permanent exhibit tracing Iowa’s African American history from Western Africa to the present.

Oral History Collection: A digital and physical collection of personal memories and narratives from African Americans in Iowa.
Traveling Exhibits: Educational resources that bring Iowa's African American history to communities across the state.

attractions.name: Amana Colonies (Amana Colonies National Historic Landmark)
attractions.category: Landmark, Historic District, Museum, and Tourist Attraction
attractions.description: The Amana Colonies are a National Historic Landmark consisting of seven scenic villages founded by German Pietists in 1855. For eighty years, the community maintained a self-sufficient communal lifestyle, and today it is a premier tourist destination celebrated for its authentic German heritage, traditional craft shops, and family-style dining (Official Website, NPS).

attractions.description: The Amana Heritage Museum tells the 300-year story of the Amana people, from their religious origins in 18th-century Germany to their communal life in the Iowa River Valley. The museum is comprised of three nineteenth-century buildings, including the 1864 Noe House and an 1870 schoolhouse, which house exhibits detailing the history, crafts, and industries of the Amana Colonies. Amana Heritage Society, National Trust for Historic Preservation

attractions.name: Coralville Dam and Lake

attractions.name: Devonian Fossil Gorge

Uncovered by major floods in 1993 and 2008, the site reveals extensive limestone bedrock filled with well-preserved fossils of ancient marine life such as corals, crinoids, and brachiopods. (USACE, University of Iowa)

attractions.name: High Amana General Store
attractions.description: Established in 1857 during the Amana communal era, the High Amana General Store is an authentic piece of history that functions as both a museum and a retail shop. The building's exterior and interior have remained virtually unchanged for over 160 years, featuring original tin ceilings, creaky wooden floors, and vintage glass display cases. Visitors can browse a unique selection of hard-to-find trinkets, handmade soaps, retro toys, and local food items in a setting that feels like stepping back in time. Amana Heritage Society, Atlas Obscura

It features a large production brewhouse, a vibrant traditional beer hall, and a family-friendly atmosphere with an emphasis on craft beer and locally sourced artisanal food. Official Website, Travel Iowa

attractions.name: Solon Beef Days Festival
attractions.description: Solon Beef Days is a community-driven festival celebrating the local agricultural heritage and beef industry. For over 50 years, this tradition has brought thousands to downtown Solon for live music, unique contests like the hay bale toss, and family-friendly activities.

attractions.name: The Coralville Strip (U.S. Route 6)
attractions.description: The Coralville Strip is a historic and bustling commercial thoroughfare located along U.S. Route 6, serving as a primary link between Coralville, the University of Iowa, and Iowa City. Known as the 'Hub of Hospitality,' it features a dense concentration of retail shops, diverse restaurants, hotels, and entertainment venues that have defined the area's growth since the 1950s. City of Coralville, Wikipedia

attractions.name: The Deadwood (also known as Deadwood Tavern)
attractions.description: The Deadwood is a beloved, long-standing neighborhood tavern in downtown Iowa City known for its diverse and welcoming atmosphere that attracts everyone from college students to local regulars. It is a classic "townie" bar that maintains a laid-back vibe with simple drink offerings, vintage amenities, and a reputation for being one of the city's most authentic dives. attractions.description: The Iowa Children’s Museum is a learning laboratory where kids can play, learn about the world around them, and create lasting memories. Its exhibits are designed with children age 12 and younger in mind, featuring hands-on educational components that promote active exploration, problem-solving, and discovery. Official Website

attractions.name: The Kalona Historical Village
attractions.description: The Kalona Historical Village is a premier destination in East-Central Iowa dedicated to exploring local heritage and Amish and Mennonite culture. The village features 12 authentic historical buildings and four curated museums that showcase the traditions, craftsmanship, and community spirit of the region. Visitors can enjoy self-guided tours of the grounds, immersive cultural displays, and streetscape recreations. Kalona Historical Village, Travel Iowa

attractions.name: The Pentacrest
attractions.description: The Pentacrest is the historic and symbolic heart of the University of Iowa, comprising a ten-acre park-like area at the center of the campus. It is home to five iconic buildings: the Old Capitol Museum, Jessup Hall, Macbride Hall, MacLean Hall, and Schaeffer Hall. This National Historic Landmark serves as a hub for education, history, and community events, offering free museums and beautifully landscaped grounds.

attractions.name: The Village Winery and Gift Gallery
attractions.description: The Village Winery is a family-owned winery and gift gallery located in the historic heart of the Amana Colonies. Specializing in traditional fruit and berry wines, the winery produces over 15 varieties on-site using recipes passed down through three generations. Visitors can enjoy complimentary tastings of unique flavors like elderberry and dandelion while browsing an extensive collection of home decor and seasonal gifts. The Village Winery, Travel Iowa

attractions.name: Thornberry Off-Leash Dog Park
attractions.description: Thornberry Off-Leash Dog Park is a premier 11-acre facility divided into specialized sections to accommodate a variety of canine needs, including a swimming pond and agility equipment. Founded in 2006 through a partnership with the Johnson County Dog Park Action Committee, it offers a safe, fenced environment with paved accessible pathways and multiple shelters for owners. attractions.name: University of Iowa Hospitals & Clinics (Medical Center University) (University of Iowa Health Care)
attractions.description: University of Iowa Health Care Medical Center is Iowa's only comprehensive academic medical center, providing a wide range of adult and pediatric primary and specialty care services. As a world-class health care institution, it serves patients from across Iowa and beyond, housing specialized centers like the Holden Comprehensive Cancer Center and the Stead Family Children's Hospital. It is a key teaching hospital for the Roy J. and Lucille A. Carver College of Medicine. (University of Iowa Health Care, Livability)

attractions.name: University of Iowa Pentacrest Museums
attractions.description: The University of Iowa Pentacrest Museums consist of the UI Museum of Natural History and the Old Capitol Museum. These institutions serve to strengthen the educational, research, and engagement missions of the University by enhancing the campus-wide focus on cultural and environmental diversity. Located at the heart of the campus, they offer a wide range of exhibits from historical artifacts to natural specimens. Pentacrest Museums

attractions.name: University of Iowa Stanley Museum of Art Wikipedia
attractions.description: The University of Iowa Stanley Museum of Art is a premier visual arts institution that serves the campus community and the state of Iowa. Established in 1969 and reopened in a new state-of-the-art building in 2022, it houses approximately 17,000 objects including world-renowned collections of African art and 20th-century Western masterpieces. The museum's mission focuses on teaching and research, offering a dynamic space for engagement with diverse global cultures Stanley Museum of Art, Wikipedia.

attractions.name: Waterworks Prairie Park
attractions.description: Waterworks Prairie Park is a 200-acre naturalized oasis in Iowa City featuring over three miles of paved trails that wind through scenic prairies and around reflective ponds. Originally a sand pit, it was acquired by the city in 2003 and now serves as a popular destination for walking, biking, and wildlife viewing. Its location just north of Interstate 80 makes it a convenient stop for travelers looking to enjoy Iowa's natural beauty and peaceful sunsets. (City of Iowa City, Airial Travel, Wikipedia)
